title: Matras Part 1: After & Below
description: In Hindi, vowels don't always stand alone — they attach to consonants as special marks called 'matras'. In this lesson, you'll learn the first five matras that appear after or below a consonant: ा (aa), ि (i), ी (ee), ु (u), and ू (oo).
keyPoints: Every consonant has a built-in 'a' sound (e.g., क = ka)
Matras replace that built-in 'a' with a different vowel sound
ा and ी attach after (to the right of) the consonant
ि is special — it is written BEFORE the consonant but pronounced after

title: The Built-in 'a' Rule
explanation: Every Hindi consonant comes with a free 'a' sound. The letter क is not just 'k' — it's 'ka'. When you want a different vowel sound, you add a matra (vowel sign) to the consonant. The matra replaces the built-in 'a'. For example, क (ka) + ा = का (kaa). Think of matras as vowel stickers you attach to consonants!
simpleRule: Consonant alone = 'a' sound. Add a matra = new vowel sound.

english: aa matra: attaches to the right of the consonant
vietnamese: Dấu nguyên âm aa: gắn bên phải phụ âm

english: i matra: written BEFORE the consonant but pronounced after it
vietnamese: Dấu nguyên âm i: viết TRƯỚC phụ âm nhưng đọc sau

english: ee matra: attaches to the right, long 'ee' sound
vietnamese: Dấu nguyên âm ee: gắn bên phải, âm 'ee' dài

english: u matra: attaches below the consonant
vietnamese: Dấu nguyên âm u: gắn bên dưới phụ âm

english: oo matra: attaches below the consonant, long 'oo' sound
vietnamese: Dấu nguyên âm oo: gắn bên dưới phụ âm, âm 'oo' dài

contextSentenceTranslation: I have to go to work.
memoryTrick: Sounds like 'calm' — you need to stay calm at work!
Notice the ि matra is written before क but you say 'ki'. किला = ki-la (fort).

contextSentenceTranslation: What is the price of this?
memoryTrick: की uses the long 'ee' matra. कीमत = kee-mat (price).
The ु matra sits below क. कुत्ता = kut-ta. Think: a cute 'kutta'!
The ू matra sits below क (longer hook than ु). कूड़ा = koo-da (trash).

prompt: Which matra is written BEFORE the consonant but pronounced after it?
subtext: Think about which matra has an unusual placement

hint: Write क and add the ा matra as a vertical line to its right. The matra looks like a standing stick: ा

subtext: Notice the ु matra below the क

title: Matras Part 1: After & Below — Recap
takeaways: Every consonant has a built-in 'a' sound; matras change it
ा (aa) — attaches to the right: क → का
ि (i) — written BEFORE the consonant: क → कि
ी (ee) — attaches to the right (long ee): क → की
Words learned: काम, किला, कीमत, कुत्ता, कूड़ा
